expectations.text: A security-report.md file is written to outputs/
Report contains an Executive Summary with severity counts
expectations.evidence: Report mentions 3 Critical, 5 High, 10 Medium, 7 Low in verdict

expectations.text: Report includes a clear production-ready verdict
expectations.evidence: Verdict: 'HIGH risk — do not deploy without fixing all CRITICAL and HIGH items'
CRITICAL-1 covers live credentials; CRITICAL-2 covers django-insecure key in git history

expectations.text: Report checks Django security settings (DEBUG, HTTPS, cookies)
expectations.evidence: HIGH-5 covers missing Django security headers; HIGH-7 covers DEBUG string bug

expectations.text: Report audits docker-compose.yml for port exposure and credentials
expectations.evidence: HIGH-1 unpinned images, HIGH-4 Redis no auth, MEDIUM covers docker-compose
15 occurrences of Remediation/Fix; ordered 10-step remediation list at the end

expectations.text: Missing security tools are documented with install instructions
expectations.evidence: Only 1 grep hit for install; no dedicated 'Missing Tools' section with install commands like the skill prescribes

summary: Baseline (without skill) was highly comprehensive — found MORE issues (3 Critical, 5 High, 10 Medium, 7 Low vs skill's 3/4/5/3) including path traversal, django-rq dashboard exposure, and .dockerignore absence. However, it did NOT produce a dedicated 'Missing Tools' section with install instructions. Report structure is less consistent. Passed 7/8 assertions.
